notes: Builds the Zoro system, then evaluates it two ways: (1) a controlled technical evaluation of 36 vibe-coding sessions across 4 conditions with rubric-scored, inter-rater-checked outcomes and ANOVA/t-tests, and (2) a qualitative user study (N=12, think-aloud + semi-structured interviews). Also includes formative interviews with 10 programmers to derive design goals. Human code is used as artifacts under test but human_subjects is true due to the participant study and interviews.
